message:
feat(extension_dispatcher): add sampled dual execution oracle with divergence-triggered rollback

Introduces a shadow dual execution subsystem that samples a configurable
fraction of extension dispatches and runs them through both the primary
and fallback paths, detecting behavioral divergence in real time:

- DualExecOracleConfig: env-configurable sample rate (PPM), divergence
  window/budget, rollback request count, and overhead budget with
  backoff — all tunable via PI_EXT_DUAL_EXEC_* environment variables
- DualExecOracleState: rolling divergence window with automatic rollback
  activation when divergence count exceeds budget within the window
- Overhead budget tracking: if shadow execution exceeds the per-request
  microsecond budget, backs off for N requests to avoid latency impact
- Rollback guard: when divergence budget is exceeded, forces fallback
  path for a configurable number of subsequent requests as a safety
  mechanism
- Full telemetry: sampled/matched/diverged/skipped counts exposed for
  observability

This enables production-safe canary validation of new extension dispatch
strategies without committing to them unconditionally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
